steps.title: Simplify the first equation to find its slope
steps.content:
Start with $16x - 20y = 6y + 8$.

Combine like terms:
$$16x - 20y - 6y = 8$$
$$16x - 26y = 8$$

Divide by 2:
$$8x - 13y = 4$$

Rewrite in slope-intercept form:
$$y = \frac{8}{13}x - \frac{4}{13}$$

The slope of the first line is $\frac{8}{13}$.

steps.title: Find the slope of the second equation in terms of t
steps.content:
From $ty = 8x + \frac{1}{5}$, divide both sides by $t$:
$$y = \frac{8}{t}x + \frac{1}{5t}$$

The slope of the second line is $\frac{8}{t}$.

steps.title: Apply the no-solution condition: set slopes equal
steps.content:
A system has no solution when the lines are parallel—same slope, different y-intercepts.

Set the slopes equal:
$$\frac{8}{13} = \frac{8}{t}$$

Since both numerators are 8:
$$t = 13$$

Verification: When $t = 13$, both lines have slope $\frac{8}{13}$ but different y-intercepts ($-\frac{4}{13}$ and $\frac{1}{65}$), confirming they are parallel with no intersection.
